design_philosophy.core_loop: Designate zones and infrastructure, manage resource flows, fulfill citizen demands, progress through historical Egyptian periods toward becoming Pharaoh
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing resource production, citizen satisfaction, defensive needs, and monument construction under limited space and economic constraints
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Rise from humble settlement administrator to Pharaoh by successfully managing increasingly complex Ancient Egyptian cities and constructing iconic monuments
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game enforces simulation-first design philosophy: all systems (housing, industry, farming, trade, religion, warfare) must be micromanaged through deliberate placement and connection. No automation. Success requires understanding systemic interactions (e.g., irrigation affecting floodplain fertility, housing improving only when all demands met). Historical authenticity prioritized where feasible (chronological Egyptian periods, accurate goods/monuments, though puzzle-like for gameplay)

difficulty.checkpointing: Campaign mode progresses through mission-based checkpoints; player must complete objective to advance to next historical period
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Free-build mode offers sandbox scenarios with optional goals; campaign mode has fixed objectives but later periods present choice between peaceful and military mission variants (only one required to progress)

progression.upgrade_system: Housing automatically improves in appearance and capability when citizen demands for goods, services, and surroundings are fulfilled; implicit city-level upgrades through improved infrastructure
progression.unlock_mechanism: Campaign progresses through five historical periods of Ancient Egypt (six with Cleopatra expansion). Completing period objectives unlocks next period and promotions up to Pharaoh rank. Later periods unlock choice between two mission types (military or peaceful)
replayability_hooks: Free-build sandbox scenarios with optional goals
Mission choice variants in later campaign periods
Achievement of different city configurations for same objectives

world_building_techniques: Historical period framing (five distinct eras of Ancient Egyptian chronology)
Appearance of historical pharaohs and leaders from actual Egyptian history
Encyclopedic information in help menu about Ancient Egyptian practices
Period-specific major events (wars, disasters, city foundings/falls)
Mission briefings contextualizing player role within historical period

faction_indicators: Pharaoh approval ('Kingdom' score replacing 'Favor') - determines if current ruler sanctions player's performance
Historical pharaohs and leaders represent different political entities player must satisfy
Trade relationships with other Ancient Egyptian cities
danger_signals: Military mission variants require warfare and defense against threats
Disease and disaster events mentioned as hazards requiring mitigation
Monument construction failures or incomplete objectives result in mission failure
environmental_storytelling: City layout reflects historical Egyptian settlement patterns
Monument construction reflects actual historical sequence of Egyptian dynasties
Nile River positioning determines agricultural viability and fishing placement
Goods availability tied to regional geographic authenticity
